disclosure: Some links are affiliate links. I only list tools I actually use — No Hype. Just the Facts.

tools.name: Apple Mac Studio
tools.tagline: The workstation behind every HNM production.
tools.description: M-series silicon, silent fans, and enough power to render, stream, code, and run a dozen AI workflows in parallel — without breaking a sweat. The daily driver.

tools.name: Bambu Lab P2S
tools.tagline: Production-grade 3D printing without the babysitting.
tools.description: Fast, multi-color, and reliable enough to run unattended. Prints functional prototypes, custom merch accessories, and brand props — same machine, same hour.

tools.name: iPhone Pro
tools.tagline: Studio in your pocket.
tools.description: The B-roll camera, scratch recorder, livestream second-screen, and on-the-go editing rig all in one. The newest Pro shoots ProRes; everything else is downstream.

tools.name: amaran
tools.tagline: Creator lighting that punches above its price.
tools.description: Aputure's creator-focused line — color-accurate LED lighting with app and Bluetooth control. Clean key light for talking-head and product shots without the cinema-rig budget.

tools.name: Elgato Stream Deck
tools.tagline: One-tap control over the whole production.
tools.description: Programmable keys that trigger scenes, mute mics, fire overlays, and launch macros mid-stream. The physical command center that keeps a live show moving without alt-tabbing.

tools.name: Ecamm
tools.tagline: Professional live streaming, Mac-native.
tools.description: Multi-scene production, guest interviews, remote callers, lower-thirds, NDI inputs. Everything you'd build in OBS, but actually pleasant to use on a Mac.

tools.name: Riverside.fm
tools.tagline: Studio-quality remote interviews.
tools.description: Each participant records locally at full quality, then the tracks are auto-uploaded and aligned. No more 'sorry, my Wi-Fi cut out' as a quality excuse.

tools.name: Descript
tools.tagline: Edit video like a doc.
tools.description: Transcription-based audio and video editing — remove filler words across an interview in one click, generate captions, rough-cut from the transcript. The biggest time-saver in the studio.

tools.name: CapCut
tools.tagline: Fast cuts for short-form.
tools.description: Reels, Shorts, TikToks — the templates, captions, and AI tools turn a 20-minute episode into a week of social cutdowns in an afternoon.

tools.name: Claude (Anthropic)
tools.tagline: The thinking partner.
tools.description: Research, scripting, strategy, content frameworks, code review. Claude handles long-context reasoning where most LLMs lose the plot — and it doesn't hallucinate citations.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.tagline: Agentic coding from the terminal.
tools.description: An autonomous coding agent that can read your repo, edit files, run commands, and ship features end-to-end. Replaced my entire 'paste-into-chat' workflow.

tools.name: Manus AI
tools.tagline: Autonomous agents that ship work.
tools.description: Multi-step task execution — research, build, deploy — without the constant babysitting. Used for one-off automation projects and weekend experiments.

tools.name: VidIQ
tools.tagline: YouTube intelligence that actually moves the needle.
tools.description: Keyword research, competitor benchmarking, thumbnail testing, and outlier discovery — the difference between guessing what to publish and knowing.

tools.name: superwhisper
tools.tagline: Voice-to-text that keeps up with your brain.
tools.description: On-device AI dictation that turns talking into clean, formatted text anywhere on the Mac. Drafts, prompts, and notes at speaking speed — and the audio never leaves your machine.

tools.name: martin
tools.tagline: A personal AI assistant that knows your day.
tools.description: A personalized AI assistant that plugs into your calendar, inbox, and tasks to handle the busywork — scheduling, reminders, and follow-ups — so the day runs without manual triage.

tools.name: littlebird
tools.tagline: AI that lives inside your daily workflow.
tools.description: An AI assistant that integrates with the tools you already work in — surfacing context, automating steps, and cutting down the app-switching that fragments a workday.

tools.name: Notion
tools.tagline: The HNM operating system.
tools.description: Weekly intel briefs, the Resource Tracker, the entire Digital Collective backend — all of it lives here. Search and structure in one place. Now the home of this very tool registry.

tools.name: Google Workspace
tools.tagline: Calendar, mail, drive — the boring essentials.
tools.description: Calendar runs the weekly rhythm. Gmail keeps the work moving. Drive holds the archive. Workhorse tier, never glamorous.

tools.name: GitHub
tools.tagline: Code, content infrastructure, and the version-controlled brain.
tools.description: Every HNM property's source lives here. The deploy pipelines run from here. Version control isn't just for developers — it's for anyone who edits anything important.

tools.name: mesh
tools.tagline: A personal CRM for the relationships that matter.
tools.description: Keeps track of the people in your network — context, last touch, and follow-ups — so important relationships don't go cold. The system behind staying genuinely connected at scale.

tools.name: Rokid
tools.tagline: AR glasses for a heads-up workflow.
tools.description: Lightweight AR glasses that float a display into your field of view — for spatial computing experiments, second-screen monitoring, and testing wearable tech before it goes mainstream.

tools.name: Ray-Ban Meta
tools.tagline: Smart glasses that actually look like glasses.
tools.description: Capture POV photo and video hands-free, take calls, and run Meta AI from your face — in a frame people don't clock as tech. The most wearable on-ramp to camera-glasses content.

tools.name: Substack
tools.tagline: Home base for the Digital Collective newsletter.
tools.description: Long-form publishing, email delivery, and subscriber relationships — all without managing a marketing stack. The current host for digitalcollective.media.

tools.name: YouTube
tools.tagline: The flagship publishing platform.
tools.description: Where the @JamesHicks channel lives — long-form video, Shorts, and the back catalog. Still the highest-leverage discovery surface for technology content.

tools.name: Fourthwall
tools.tagline: Merch infrastructure that just runs.
tools.description: Storefront, payments, fulfillment, custom domain — all out of the box. Powers merch.hicksnewmedia.com. Zero ops overhead means more time on the products themselves.

tools.name: Captions.ai
tools.tagline: AI-powered short-form, start to finish.
tools.description: Auto-captions, AI editing, eye-contact correction, and AI avatars for short-form. Turns raw talking-head footage into platform-ready clips fast.

tools.name: DualShot Recorder
tools.tagline: Front and back camera, recorded at once.
tools.description: Records both iPhone cameras at once — your reaction and the subject in a single take. The fast way to capture POV and reaction content without a multi-cam rig.
